title: Scroll Markers, Organic Bento, Living Ink, if() & Master Clock
description: Five 2026 CSS-feature drops: native ::scroll-marker carousels, shape() organic bento, random() jittered type, if() adaptive buttons, and a progress()-driven master-clock timeline.

drops.title: Zero-JS Native Carousel
drops.description: A CSS-only carousel with browser-generated nav arrows and pagination dots — no Swiper, no Slick.
drops.concept: CSS Overflow Module 5 introduces ::scroll-button() and ::scroll-marker pseudo-elements that let the browser itself generate carousel controls from a plain scroll-snap container. It landed in Chrome/Edge 135 and Safari 18.2, crossing ~75% global support by mid-2026 and triggering a wave of "retire your slider library" posts.
drops.source: Giorgio Saud — "CSS Carousels 2026: Native Scroll Markers Finally Here"

interaction.mouse: Click the arrows or dots, or drag to scroll
interaction.click: Toggle autoplay and speed in the tuning panel
interaction.touch: Swipe the track; dots and arrows are touch-sized

drops.title: Organic Bento with shape()
drops.description: A bento grid where cells morph into responsive, hover-reactive organic blobs instead of rectangles.
drops.concept: clip-path: shape() reached Baseline across engines in February 2026 — the first CSS clipping syntax with true curves (curve to / arc commands) that accepts %, rem and calc() instead of SVG's fixed-pixel viewBox, making "beyond rectangles" bento layouts finally responsive by construction.

interaction.mouse: Hover a blob cell to see it pop into an alternate shape
interaction.touch: Tap a blob cell to trigger the morph

drops.title: Living Ink
drops.description: Every headline letter gets its own stable, organic rotation and drift — hand-set type, not uniform digital text.
drops.concept: Safari 26.5 refined CSS random() with an element-scoped keyword, letting random(--r element-scoped, -4deg, 4deg) draw an independent value per matched element instead of one shared value across all of them — unlocking per-letter randomness without a JS Math.random() loop.

interaction.mouse: Hover the headline to intensify the jitter
interaction.click: Regenerate the pattern or tune intensity
interaction.touch: Tap the headline; use the panel to tune

drops.title: Input-Aware Buttons
drops.description: One button set whose size, shimmer, and press feedback all branch on pointer type — no duplicated media-query blocks.
drops.concept: CSS if() (inline media()/style()/supports() conditionals) is the feature every "2026 CSS you must know" roundup is currently pushing as the thing that finally collapses duplicated @media(hover:hover)/@media(pointer:coarse) rule blocks into single declarations.
drops.source: Chrome for Developers — "CSS conditionals with the new if() function"

interaction.mouse: Hover for the shimmer sweep; click a variant to switch shape
interaction.click: Toggle "simulate touch device" to see sizing adapt
interaction.touch: Tap for press feedback; sizing auto-adapts on real touch devices

drops.title: Master-Clock Timeline
drops.description: One shared 0→1 clock drives five overlapping, independently-windowed element animations — GSAP's core idea, in CSS.
drops.concept: CSS progress() returns a normalized 0-1 ratio between two bounds and works as a unit-agnostic interpolator inside calc(). Using it to give each element its own custom clock-window (rather than a single fade) rebuilds GSAP's signature "one timeline, many position-parameterized tweens" natively, with zero animation-delay drift.
drops.source: Amit Merchant — "The new progress() function in CSS"

interaction.mouse: Drag the scrub slider to manually move the shared clock
interaction.click: Toggle play/pause and adjust loop speed
interaction.touch: Drag the scrub slider with a finger
